mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly increasing present values of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of rate-cut periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real personal spending and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in spending and housing starts fails to produce a positive 60-day return in at least 50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.4 percent are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumption capacity, directly boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods yield negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real personal spending, where spending decelerates while GDP remains elevated, indicates weakening household demand that precedes negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spending-GDP divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase confirms supply-side normalization, reducing recession fears and triggering positive short-term equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration in recovery phases fails to generate positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of historical cases.
